name: ION-14382 Booking Email Test Content Verification Review
description: Review of email test changes in the AWS SDK v2 upgrade PR. Analyzing loss of email content verification (HTML/text body assertions) in OutBoundEmailSenderTest and LegalTermsEmailTest, and proposing strategies to restore it using the new cloud-sdk-aws EmailService API.

entries.summary: Identified 2 test files with email content verification removed; ~28 test methods total lost subject/HTML/text assertions.
entries.detail:
Analyzed the full-diff.patch for email test changes. Two main test files had email content verification removed:

1. OutBoundEmailSenderTest.java — ~20 test methods affected. Previously used `verify(rawEmailSender).sendEmail(new EmailContent(...subject, htmlContent, textContent), vars)` to assert exact email subject, HTML body, and text body. Now replaced with `verify(emailService).sendTemplateEmail(anyString(), anyList(), any(Map.class))` — only verifying the method was called, not its arguments.

2. LegalTermsEmailTest.java — ~8 test methods affected. Same pattern: previously verified full EmailContent (subject, HTML, text) + template vars via rawEmailSender. Now only verifies emailService.sendTemplateEmail is called with the correct template name and recipient list.

Other files (OutboundServiceTest, OutboundServiceAddnlTest, OutboundServiceImplTest, OutboundServiceTestBase, OutboundResourceTest) only had type renames (EmailSenderConfig → BookingEmailConfig) — no email content verification was present in those files before.

Model: Claude Opus 4.6

entries.summary: EmailSendResult.emailRequest contains rendered contentHtml/contentText. Content verification IS possible but requires different approach since EmailService is mocked in unit tests.
entries.detail:
Critical finding: The new cloud-sdk-aws EmailService.sendTemplateEmail() returns EmailResultCollector<EmailSendResult>, and EmailSendResult contains an EmailRequest field. EmailRequest has contentHtml and contentText fields that hold the rendered template output.

In SesEmailServiceImpl.processSuccessResult(), the EmailRequest is constructed from TemplateContent which holds the fully-rendered Handlebars HTML and text. This means the rendered email content IS accessible in the result — via emailSendResult.getEmailRequest().getContentHtml() and getContentText().

However, in the tests, emailService is MOCKED, so sendTemplateEmail returns a pre-built mock collector. The real rendering never happens in unit tests.

Two approaches to restore content verification:
1. ArgumentCaptor on vars: Capture the Map<String, Object> vars passed to sendTemplateEmail. Verify that the template variable map contains the correct values (subject, carrier, shipper, etc). This validates the converter logic but NOT the template rendering.
2. Integration-style: Use real EmailTemplatesLoader + HandlebarsTemplateServiceImpl to render the template with captured vars, then assert on the rendered HTML/text. This is closer to the old tests but requires the template files on classpath.

Model: Claude Opus 4.6

entries.summary: Analysis complete. Recommended Strategy B: real SesEmailServiceImpl + mocked SesV2Client to restore full email content verification in tests.
entries.detail:
Created comprehensive analysis document at booking/docs/2026-03-24-email-test-review-summary.md.

Key findings:
1. 2 test files affected: OutBoundEmailSenderTest.java (~20 methods) and LegalTermsEmailTest.java (~8 methods) lost email content verification
2. 8 additional removed tests were legacy email-sender internals (RawEmailSender, HandlebarsTemplateEmailSender, filterInvalidAddress) — deletion justified
3. 6 other test files only had type renames (EmailSenderConfig → BookingEmailConfig)
4. 49 expected-output resource files (expected_*_email.html/txt) still exist but are now unused

Recommendation: Restore content verification using Strategy B — real SesEmailServiceImpl with mocked SesV2Client. This provides full content verification equivalent to old tests, reuses existing expected-output resource files, and primarily requires changes only in setUp() methods. EmailSendResult.emailRequest contains rendered contentHtml/contentText/subject from the real Handlebars rendering pipeline.

Model: Claude Opus 4.6

entries.summary: Agent using Claude Opus 4.6 for this session.
